title: Conventional Retail Method
event: Hollis Co.'s records show: beginning inventory $14,000 cost / $20,000 retail; purchases $86,000 cost / $120,000 retail; net markups $10,000; net markdowns $5,000; sales $130,000. Compute ending inventory at cost under the conventional (LCM) retail method.

computationPaths.narration: Conventional retail computes the cost-to-retail ratio INCLUDING markups but EXCLUDING markdowns — that lowers the ratio and approximates lower-of-cost-or-market.

memorize.body: Conventional ratio = cost ÷ (retail + markups), markdowns EXCLUDED from the ratio but subtracted to reach EI at retail.
Markdowns appear once (reaching EI retail), never twice — including them in the ratio converts the method from LCM to cost.

questions.prompt: Ratio base: markdowns excluded → 66.67%. EI at retail $15,000. Ending inventory at cost = ?

distractors.feedback: You included the 5,000 markdowns in the ratio denominator (100/155 = 64.5%). Conventional EXCLUDES markdowns from the ratio: 66.67% × 15,000 = 10,000.
That's ending inventory at RETAIL. Convert to cost with the ratio: 10,000.
